reviews.review_text:
I'm legitimately only leaving a review to respond to WumpusGaming, as there's no other way to address how objectively abysmal his review is.

"I cannot get Enshrouded to run after the last patch without it using 80-100% of the GPU and heating up to 40-50C. Every single other game I own uses between 20-40% of the GPU and heats up to 30-40C."

Steam should ban you from leaving future reviews after typing that. High GPU utilization is most often a good thing and it means you're not bottlenecked elsewhere. If you're used to 20-40%, it's because you're playing some 16-bit pixel graphics and/or have low FPS caps set, because I doubt you're CPU bottlenecked with a 7950X3D. The cherry on top is that you're leaving a thumbs down because your GPU is at 40-50C, which is cool and well within rated temps, by the way. 

"The game is basically unplayable now and its sad."

It's actually disgusting that Steam allows reviews like this and there's no way to reply to them directly. Comments should not be optional. This is either malicious, or you're so confused that I don't know how you dress yourself in the morning.

The Game is good if you like ARPG's 

-There is a lot of depth to the combat but you have to go through the skill tree to add that depth via the newly unlocked combat skills that allow more special moves. 

-The map is massive 
-The Building is a lot of fun and a huge variety of different styles
-You can scale the difficulty, Quantity of enemies, xp gain, time it takes to mine resources 
-Coop is a blast 
-The travel mechanic is gliding which is a lot of fun and the glider can be upgraded 
-There is puzzles via the Spires/Towers 

I easily lost 200 hours before writing this,
Check reviews, watch game play and check feed back to read up on what others found negative about the game. 

Me Personally, love it and my experience has been great. 

I am going to easily pour another 200 hours in, the building alone is a lot of fun.

Its great fun exploring, doing combat and building with friends.
i also like the visual style and graphics overal!

BUT, i was playing and (almost randomly) with a recent update i think, my overall fps went from 80 - 100 fps at 1440p. to about 50 - 60 with more stuttering and frametime dips/spikes. 

my biggest wish is for a performance update right now.


In the end its still a very enjoyable game so thumbs up from me!

I know its early access, but you cant have your final boss the Dragon (the corrupted beast) reset the fight at 10% hp after fighting it for 20 minutes. This happend several times ... and we gave up. It's kinda gamebreaking.

EVERY time now, when i want to play the game, it needs to build/verify the shaders....While you can start playing, it always makes the game laggy and at night it makes light sources non existent until its finished.
THIS NEEDS ADRESSING! No other game does this, get this finally fixed. 
It is a nice game, but this problem makes it borderline, as everytime i want to spin it up, it needs to build the shaders first...

100 Hours Later...

Enshrouded is one of the few survival-crafting games that kept me engaged for over 100 hours.

The exploration is fantastic, the world is beautiful, and the building system is easily one of the best in the survival genre. The freedom to create detailed structures and customize your base is what kept me playing long after completing most of the main content.

Combat and progression are enjoyable, and the developers have done a great job supporting the game with regular updates.

However, there are still areas that could be improved. Late-game combat can become repetitive, enemy variety is somewhat limited, and loot progression could use more depth. As someone who spends a lot of time building, I also wish the game had a free-build camera mode. Constructing large or detailed projects would be much more convenient if builders could move the camera independently without having to position their character every time.

Despite these shortcomings, Enshrouded already offers an impressive amount of content and one of the best building experiences available in a survival RPG.

What I'd like to see in the future:

1. Free-build camera mode
2. More enemy variety
3. Deeper loot and endgame progression
4. More building quality-of-life features

Would be an amazing game if the combat wasn't balanced by a random number generator and had hitboxes that made any kind of sense. Some regular enemy attacks deal literally 10x the damage of others while being exactly the same speed. Some enemies of the exact same level and character model deal triple the damage of others for absolutely no reason. You will frequently be killed while standing behind an enemy while it attacks forwards. The greatsword heavy attack is completely useless if you are on a wooden platform because it's limited to one target and will hit the platform you're standing on before anything else, disabling the hitbox. Against some enemies the lockon feature will target 10 feet below them making all your attacks miss. Etc etc etc.

It kind of doesn't matter how many cool systems you have feeding into your combat when you can't be bothered to make the combat system not complete ass. Unless the 1.0 release of the game fixes many, many issues with it, cannot recommend unless you just want to play on easy difficulty and mess around building bases.

Overall the game can be really fun and engaging. The world is large and interesting with lots to explore. Building is fantastic, the voxel system is amazing. Generally speaking the game is great. 

Except, it's extremely clunky and feels like a lot of the designs were put in place solely to be infuriating. 

There a multiple types of "ground" that if you touch, you basically sink into them and instantly die. In a lot of cases these are used as design elements in the level design to be....annoying? I don't even know. Half the time this happens you can't recover your body and lose all your stuff because your corpse will spawn right beside it way down a hole that is impossible to get out of. 

The whole world is designed with height in mind yet there is no acutal grappling hook or way to "go up" making getting around very annoying. Coupled with the weird momentum based movement that randomly changes speed for no reason, you often fall off stuff and then can't get back up.

Combat is really clunky, most enemies has no stagger and just power through everything while you get stuck in long animations. 

Progression is all over the place with all items being RNG. Exploring the area you're "leveled" to often results in mostly useless items.

I hope it improves but the core problems imo are foundational to the engine and the game. While I have enjoyed playing it, I find myself quitting more and more often as I get really frustrated with the clunky mechanics.

Has some flaws but overall fun game. 

Nice that you can heavily customize the difficulty to your tastes. The gliding and digging mechanics make exploring very easy and rewarding. Static respawn chest locations when the server restarts means you can cheese the item progression if you want to spend more timing exploring and building than fighting. The base building is top-notch and has so many options combining prebuilt objects and parts with the ability to literally place one block at a time minecraft style if that's what you're into.

My main complaint is the animation locking and 3rd person camera control during fighting fields clumsy at times.  The blue mist vs red mist mechanic is also a bit annoying at first and feels heavy-handed but once you get a better glider and pickaxe it's actually really fun to try to subvert the red mist and the yellow button puzzle systems but digging and flying your way past them.
The game is solid, but the jumping puzzles and fetch quests suck.

I don't know, the game overall seems interesting - it feels like Valheim + modern Assassins Creed. You get huge map with lots of question marks with different places and activities behind them, you run around exploring them, fight, gather resources, then go back to your base to build/craft a little bit and repeat.

It plays kinda good, looks great, and there's some space for character building too.

BUT. But... first of all - it's very repetitive. Last 6 hours I spent there was just finding some trash in random chests and fight all the same enemies.

Second of all - the technical state of the game is weirdest I ever seen. I have a great PC, the game runs smoothly on best quality with all optional stuff on. No problems with FPS. BUUUUT. VFXs sometimes just disappear for couple minutes. I don't understand what is this, never seen anything like that. The game just stops showing projectiles, AOE zones of skills and bombs, light sources, light from your torch! Which can kill you if you're in a dark place or just at night. And sometimes it gets even funnier - after these couple minutes with no VFXs they start to rapidly spawn all at once for last like 30 seconds. It looks like you're playing some MMO with a 56k dial-up modem or smth, and server just finally gave you responses for all your packages you've sent last minute all at once. It's the weirdest ♥♥♥♥ I ever seen, considering I'm playing a private solo game, so there's no server, and why only VFXs then? Everything else works perfectly - enemies throw bombs with AOEs I don't see, archers and mages are throwing their projectiles at you which you can't see either, so you die coz you don't see what to dodge. This is just such a mess.

I'd say if you really want to play proper survival-craft - there're some much less repetitive and fun to explore games. If you want to run around between some question marks - just play Assassins Creed I guess, there's at least some cutscenes, historical settings and dialogues. Enshrounded gives you only notes notes notes like some 2010 indie horror. BUT - the game looks amazing. I keep getting back to it just because of the looks really, but then get tired in couple of hours.

Every time I launch this game I am forced to compile shaders and it takes VERY long. It ruined the whole game for me. My friends have the same problem, so at the worsed we had to wait an hour before everyone was done compiling...

Enshrouded : A Promising Foundation Buried Under Frustration
Some call it the best survival game, others the best action RPG. Few games have left me as conflicted as Enshrouded.
Don't get me wrong, Enshrouded has genuine qualities. But it also carries a weight of frustrations and missed opportunities that are hard to ignore.

- Combat : Souls-Inspired But Poorly Executed
The combat system clearly draws inspiration from souls-likes, but feels unfinished and archaic even after the recent combat update.
Melee is at a severe disadvantage compared to ranged, not just in power, but how it feels. Every action, whether attacking or dodging, locks you into a 0.5+ second animation with no way to cancel. Getting surrounded by three enemies means getting infinitely staggered while your own attacks don't stagger them at all.
The parry window is too lenient, you can parry on the very first frame and it always works, which makes it feel cheap rather than satisfying. The dodge on the other hand feels half-broken, with i-frames that seem to exist on paper only, you'll take damage mid-roll constantly.
Heavy attacks hitting only a single target even when enemies are standing side by side is baffling. The camlock takes ages to reorient when an enemy moves behind you. Enemy AI is inconsistent, some won't detect you standing right in front of them, even hitting them while others get stuck in walls and fall through the floor when hit.
Daggers are arguably the worst offender, weak, with a single animation, and stealth as a playstyle is essentially non-existent.
Ranged combat avoids most of these issues but becomes incredibly monotonous, you'll repeat the exact same actions for dozens of hours with no variation. This was a major complaint at launch and unfortunately remains unchanged.
You can't hold left click to attack continuously, which is particularly painful when playing with a wand. You also can't hold E to loot you'll be mashing your key to collect 100 plants one by one.
The enemy variety is disappointing, you'll encounter the same enemies from the beginning all the way to level 35+, even if the bestiary becomes slightly more varied in later areas

- Gathering & Exploration : Tedious And Unrewarding
Chopping wood is painful. You're animation-locked for the entire duration, it's not satisfying, and the inconsistency is maddening, why do I have to manually pick up wood but not ore when mining? Mining is slightly better but still lacks impact and feels unrewarding.
Exploration is centered entirely around the glider, which works well enough, but the absence of mounts means you'll spend a lot of time simply running. The lack of a climbing system is a missed opportunity that would have made the world feel far more dynamic.
Points of interest are hit or miss, many feel empty or pointless, and some are outright copy-pasted, including certain elixir wells that share the same layout with chests spawning in the exact same spot.

- Atmosphere & Visuals : A Genuine Highlight
This is where Enshrouded genuinely shines. The world is beautiful across every biome, forests, snow, underwater. I've found myself stopping multiple times just to take in the atmosphere, especially in forested areas.
The weather system is alive and dynamic, particularly in snowy regions. However, it rains almost constantly, and the endurance debuff it applies nearly every day gets old fast.
Night has a fantastic atmosphere and even offers exclusive resources, but it's undermined by near-total darkness with no real solution. You can carry a torch, but you can't fight with one in hand. A belt lantern would have been an obvious and elegant fix.
The Shroud is the biggest visual offender. Visibility is already poor, but pulling out a torch in there effectively flashbangs you. The blue visual effects add to the blindness more than the atmosphere.
The art direction overall is excellent, environments, enemies, armor sets, weapons and cosmetics all look astonishing.

- Crafting & Base Building : Deep But Overwhelming
Enshrouded arguably has the best base building in its genre in terms of sheer possibility, a massive variety of materials, pieces and customisation options. I do miss the structural integrity system from Valheim, but that's a personal preference.
That said, the depth comes at a cost. The number of different workshops is overwhelming, and it's genuinely confusing to remember which one does what. Workshops don't pull from nearby chests, which means constant manual inventory management. Upgrading a chest requires emptying it, removing it, and replacing it, a baffling design choice.
The sheer number of different resources compounds this, you'll regularly feel lost in your own inventory. Crafting from your inventory, from NPCs and from various workshops simultaneously creates redundancy without clarity.
The building process itself is clunky, you craft pieces at a workbench, then switch to your hammer, scroll through options and select the right material. It works but it's a headache.

- Quests & Progression : Fedex With Style
The NPC quest system is extensive, which is genuinely useful for players who don't enjoy pure sandbox gameplay, it gives you a clear direction to follow. But it quickly becomes repetitive and Ubisoft-like in its structure.
The gameplay loop overall lacks depth and variety. It's enjoyable at first but fades into repetition sooner than it should.
On the positive side, the talent tree is varied and readable, and the ability to reset your stats easely to experiment with different builds is a great quality of life feature.
Loot is decent, legendaries appear regularly without completely invalidating other rarities, which keeps things balanced.

- Hollow Halls : The Best Part Of The Game
The dungeons called Hollow Halls are genuinely impressive. Large, dense with enemies and full of atmosphere. Walking into my first skeleton invasion, I felt like I was in the scene from Lord of the Rings where Aragorn, Legolas and Gimli face the Army of the Dead. That's a compliment.
The level 35 Hollow Hall though... let's just say it's a bit of a middle finger and leave it at that.

Final thoughts
Enshrouded has real strengths, stunning visuals, excellent art direction, deep base building, solid RPG systems and a massive amount of content. But it's weighed down by combat that lacks depth and polish, overwhelming crafting systems, tedious gathering, and a gameplay loop that runs out of steam too quickly.
The game is noticeably more enjoyable in co-op, which helps paper over some of its weaknesses.
There's a genuinely great game somewhere in here. It's just not fully there yet.

Was hoping this could be a fun game for my friends and I to play together, but the forced shared progression really ruins it for us. We have disparate schedules and some folks prefer to spend more time crafting while others prefer to spend more time adventuring, but the current system punishes players unless they are all online at the same time AND keep their characters close to each other. If another character completes a quest and you weren't standing right next to them, you miss out on that and the corresponding experience. I've never played a multiplayer survival game so unaccommodating to parallel play and disparate gaming schedules. I haven't been able to play for a week now because one of my friends has been too busy and I don't want to ruin their quest progress. At this point it just makes more sense to turn off the server, uninstall the game, and find a game that will  how and when we can play together.

I know everyone and their dog are in love with this game. Frankly, I am not sure why.
I found it to be a long stream of small annoyances and frustrations.

Great game, I still have so much more to go. lots of content. 
Graphics are great, 
storeline is well done, 
Combate is interesting, I'm not great at combate but this game is definetly helping me get better,
Crafting can be a bit annoying as you have to go to specific people to do certain things.
I enjoy the skill tree and the game is pretty good at slowing down your progress to make it harder to speed run the game

My buddy baited me to get this game, I got it and I regret it so much. My main issues are the unsatisfactory combat, having to wait 10 minutes each time I load the game up so I don't get 2 frames while paying it, the boring variety of enemies, and the progression is super slow. To me this game just does not feel special. I tried to give this game multiple chances and I regretted it each time.

I want to recommend this game. But I cannot. I have 64 GB of RAM, a Ryzen 9 CPU, and a AMD Sapphire 7900 XTX GPU and I cannot get Enshrouded to run after the last patch without it using 80-100% of the GPU and heating up to 40-50C. Every single other game I own uses between 20-40% of the GPU and heats up to 30-40C. I don't know what they did to kill such a good game with that last patch but no matter what tweaks I make to graphics or the AMD Adrenaline Software has solved the issue. The game is basically unplayable now and its sad. 

If it weren't for that I would recommend Enshrouded because its a great combo of lite ARPG mechanics and a survival crafting game all rolled into one. The building is great, the combat is fun, the graphics and music are good. But good god is the game resource intensive.

i love this game so much but there are some decisions that are just baffling to me that make this impossible for me to recommend. its really fun to explore when i can actually see the interesting things in the distance, but after the first 2 areas if feels like they start hiding every poi behind some sorta choke point that forces you to follow a very specific path to find, which is only a minor headache until they start treating entrances to the shroud the same way. when youre on a time limit to find a way out that little design quirk can be infuriating. i think its cool that theyll give you tips on where to find missing crafting materials, but thats really only a bandaid for the fact that they never seem to explain things. like early on its not a big deal, obviously wood comes from trees and honey comes from bees, but then you start needing a buncha things that arent really comparable to anything in real life, and they just dont really give you enough info to know where to find it. this game is way too new to already need a guide. that ♥♥♥♥♥ reserved for games with years upon years of updates to have to learn.

now, if you purely intend to build things here and dont care about the other aspects, then i couldnt recommend it more. easily the best building system ive seen in a video game.

It's fine, I guess? Platforming isn't great, the build system is pretty clunky, combat feels okay. Everything is almost there and I'm worried that there's too much "new" stuff and not enough refining old stuff. Hold off from me until we see where it ends.

Everything else in this game is OK, except for the Fell Dragon fight is VERY bad. It goes back up to full health at about one-third health every time i fight it. I am ALWAYS doing constant damage with stun and steel arrows inside the ring but nothing I do can take it down to zero as it just flies back up at will to replenish health.

Everything is ok I guess, the level jumps for the items are sometimes abit crazy when going into new areas. Still no love for frontliners, melee drops. The young fell dragon is too glitchy (health goes back to full/randomly gets stuck) after all that reflecting and too troublesome to fight, its not even fun. the bosses gets even more boring as you progress this game

I cannot recommend this game in its current form. Crashes constantly, even after a reinstall and making sure my video drivers are up to date. This is on a fairly good gaming system too. There's no way it should be this unstable.

Game is interesting at first, but then becomes extremely boring and repetitive. The constant having to wait 10-15 minutes for shader recompiles made me uninstall. What an absolute technical blunder that sucks all the fun out of wanting to sit down and just play a game.

Lots of potential, but...

There's a lot of basic stuff that's either implemented poorly or missing.
Combat feels weightless, movement is floaty and clunky at the same time. Not sure if this is intended or if changes are planned.

Building system is so full of potential, but with lots of annoying quirks like not having drag-to-build or logical surface snapping. Blocks seem to snap to an awkward "world grid" instead of each other.

I have 3-4 hours of free time per day TOPS to play something. I don't even play games daily sometimes, so when I do play something, it's supposed to be at least somewhat meaningful.
This game is frustrating.
The map feels like the game wants to give you a climbing option, yet you have none.
The combat has potential, but it's full of shortcomings even with the last update as of the time of the review.
The graphics and the performance is god-awful and is lacking despite the game having textures of inconsistent quality and the only good thing is the shading and the lighting (which is probably the main cause of performance being bad).
The game's progression system is awkward and unintuitive. You have a ton of different work stations, a ton of quests to upgrade them, yet you don't feel like you accomplish something. You just feel like nothing's changed.
The gear progression is even more awkward. Yeah, okay, I can craft armor. But why can't I craft weapons then? Why should I search for weapons Fortnite-style and "upgrade" them with coins and whatnot?
Sometimes I feel like asking "why can't you just be normal?" but even if I ask this I understand that there will be no answer.
The game is full of what-ifs and if-onlys. 
But what I get is another shader loading downtime.
Every update released forces the game's shaders to reset, which in turn forces your PC to reload them, which takes about 20-30 minutes on a decent PC (specs included in the review). 
Do I feel like wasting half an hour waiting for the game to load half it's graphics? I am playing as a mage, I literally can neither see my attacks nor the parry signals from enemies before the shaders've finished compiling. But after those 20-30 minutes I'm already super-tired of the game and I just quit it.

Game's specific mechanics aside, it objectively needs two things:
A climbing mechanic
A performance overhaul

Until then, consider that you won't be able to see a 'Recommend' review until your shaders finish compiling.

One of the best games I've ever played.

What I love most about Enshrouded is the building system and the incredible freedom it gives you. You can create anything from a massive castle to a small, cozy house filled with detailed decorations. The amount of customization is amazing, and there are countless ways to design and decorate your base exactly the way you want.

The storytelling is also perfect for this type of game. There are almost no cutscenes (except for one at the very beginning), and you don't have an NPC constantly explaining everything to you. Instead, you discover the story through books, notes, and exploration. The more you explore, the more pieces of the story you uncover, gradually revealing what happened to the world. This approach creates a unique atmosphere and makes the world feel much more immersive.

Speaking of the world, Embervale is absolutely beautiful. The graphics are stunning, and the environment feels both atmospheric and cozy. At the same time, the Shroud adds a darker side to the experience, transforming once-beautiful areas into mysterious and unsettling places.

Overall, I highly recommend Enshrouded to everyone. Whether you enjoy building, exploring, or uncovering lore, this game has something special to offer. You're truly missing out if you haven't given Enshrouded a try.

I can hardly wait for the full release and can't wait to see what the developers have in store for the future of this amazing game.

It's tough when asked to give a recommendation for a game that recently updated and changed the way you feel about it.

I'm going to recommend, because it is still a great game that ticks a lot of boxes, particularly if you are after a survival light adventure rpg with a lot of interesting mechanics and content (particularly when you realise it is still in early access). The positives are clear, the team is dedicated to creating a great game and there is no sign they are not giving it a good crack. However, despite initially being excited for the latest "Forging the Path" update, I quickly felt that the latest update just took some of instinctive gameplay mechanics that I loved out, in order to raise the skill bar and add more depth to class builds.

I don't want to sell the latest updates short. Specifically, for me, with the loss of the quick-shot mechanics, the current limited npc utility (I know this will be improved), and the push toward more specialised class builds - I say this because I felt the previous gear and build mechanics rewarded mixing and matching in a way that allowed for hybrid builds to be very compelling -  has left me with no desire to continue the adventure at this point.  I have nothing but appreciation for the journey that was on offer when I put time into the game.

This game is seriously so enjoyable. It just has it all. The building, the exploration, the combat, the story and RPG element. It just does so many things so well, and the dev team listens to input and post updates on their YouTube channel. 

I'm losing sleep over this game already. 10/10.
Fun game. until the dragon. Its the worst boss fight ever. And glitchy as hell.

Don't have to much time, but I wanted to give my first impression.
So far I did my first Altar upgrade and started getting good weapons and different class options. I gotta say, outside of the almost useless Grappling hook? The game is lots of fun. For me to give it a Full, 100% recommendation? Make that Hook able to grab onto basic terrain to scale mountains and out of shroud territory easier just by going up. Right now I have to mine myself steps using the pickaxe when i'm in deep doo doo. Please, for the love of god fix that because the game would be perfect.
Enjoyable game, what it doesn't tell you just google or you can get frustrated with cryptic clues

Enshrouded has the all features a survival game needs. Has great map and landscapes.Also you will find suprising things everywhere you go. For crafting : I think it's difficult if you wanna build beautiful and realistic structures because building menu is complex. So buy and play , you will have great gameplay experience

simply amazing been a long time since i have played a game this diverse especially considering its only early access really been enjoying this gem and will 100% recommend this to anyone.  Ps keep up the good work you creator's and team and thank you.
Absolutely in love with the scenery in visuals of the game it’s amazing to play night or day amazing lighting building farms and exploring waters is super fun also
great game, great graphics, everything is fantastic.

This is a great game, its a lot of fun. You have to go in knowing what it is, and what it isn't. There is numerous skill trees and you can re-spec. There are lots of crafters for your base and lots of functional crafts as well as flavor stuff for all of your building needs. The world tells a story sort of like a dark souls game in a way that the lore is sprinkled around in books, scrolls, quests, etc. Lots of different build choices as well. I really enjoy it so far :)
очень затягивает. очень красиво. отличные головоломки. строительство базы интереснее чем где-либо ещё. дивной красоты музыка. и нормальный интерфейс!

i like the game so far and the building system  but the exp;oration is bad and super slow ,i waste half of my time walking on foot for one mission then i died right away and i have to loop this for 2 times,
the glider dosen't save that much time , the game wont let you move freely or take a shortcut
you are forced to follow the one and only long boring path 
i think the game could really use a mounts update 
7/10 6/10?
could be higher but yeah 
till the next update i guess?
buy in sale

Played 95 hours before the Forge the Path Update and it was really fun. 
Now, after the Update is must say ... it only gets better in my opinion. 
Huge improvements on the skill tree and also the devs listen to the community.
If you like playing an archer, don't play this game. Class is weak af after the last update.

I love playing this game with my kids on the relaxed settings.  We've put many hours in the game just exploring and building.  The only major gripe I have is the Fell Dragon fight is pretty ridiculous and was designed poorly.  (I know...skill issue, right?)  Minor issues: 1. Optimization - gotta crank the fans up! 2. My son's pc is running bazzite and the shaders take forever to load.  Could be the pc itself, but something to be aware of.
I played this game last year and now that I'm wanting to play it again, the entire animation changed! Why do I look like a dwarf child? What in the world happened!?

"lets take every possible design decision and turn it into a game!"
-can't do anything at night, everything has to stop because it gets so dark you can't see anything
-only 2 or 3 working classes, the rest will get oneshoted by anything at any point of the game
-after dying about 100 times to a boss as a mage, I reespec my character into a melee one and 2 shoted the boss even with trash gear(wtf) i didn't even lost hp
-the game applyed me a debug of 2 hours, 2 REAL hours that stops me from going into the shroud areas, 2 REAL HOURS!!!! and there's no way to remove it lol
-huge maps, you can't sprint much because stamina is not existent 
bad game design

I love this game. Really do. But the combat update broke the game for me. Hard crashes after no more the 15 minutes now every time. Something about a memory dump. It's unfortunate because I really like the game. But it's going on 3 weeks of it being unplayable.

Im not sure, it has potential, theres a whole lot going on. And while I think thats great, nothing is really special, or memorable. Combat is janky, building is janky, its fun but nothing groundbreaking.

There's no FPS limiter, so I have to listen to my PC destory itself while I play.
Definitely worth waiting for this issue to be resolved, unless you have a way of limiting FPS external to the game

I want to recommend this game badly. It's my favorite survival/multiplayer game. But the compiling shaders made it that one of my friends quit, he got through it twice, but the third time: a small patch after a bigger path, was just too much. He has a decently good laptop but the compiling shaders take up to hours, which isn't even a guarantee cause it can still crash. I really hope they'll end up fixing this.

I don't recommend this game unless they radically change the gameplay.
The game starts slow and boring, improves after a few hours, but eventually it all devolves into mindless monotony and grinding. Most of the time you have to farm tons of resources, especially if you're playing with someone, or just wait for items to cook at workbenches. These and all the other developers who were smart enough to add timers for cooking items, and such long timers at that, should go make stupid mobile games instead of proper PC games—that's where they belong, in the trash. Even the fact that you can set resources and time to 2x doesn't help. They just drag out the gameplay with their timers; it's utter idiocy.
The combat system is too broken and dull. To find decent items for combat, you have to explore the entire map, since you can only craft clothes. Half the mage's spells are either buggy or useless and don't improve as the world progresses. I don't know if the developers just hate mages or just don't know what they're doing.

So, the game is already two years old, and there have been almost no improvements. The game is a boring, broken piece of crap.

I used to be able to play this game on my PC and I played a good amount of it. Then the updated made it nearly impossible to play. I get that the game gets new updates and overhauls and stuff like that, but now I can't play anymore due to the required memory on the game going up. I get less then 15fps with the lowest graphical setting which isn't playable. It sucks because before all the updates I put in over 10 hours which means I can no longer even get a refund.
There is just too much game. You spend an hour playing just to find a piece of gear thats 1% better.

The game is fun with a lot of things to do, but i will not progress or play it anymore until there are tools for verticality/climbing etc, i am sick and tired of rotating through half the map when im 200 meters from the objectives, or trying to dig up to it, for my pick to brake, then go repair it and go back to find the wholes have recovered :D Also add more space in inventory since the start of the game, too many things to gather and carry even in the first zone.
Encorporates so many other survival games features but still feels very orignal, the combat is amazing the world, the progression truley a peak survial game
An immersive game that offers story-line, base building, survival and pleasing environmental landscapes.

I would love to recommend this game, but every single update AND hotfix forces a 30+ Minute wait as it compiles shaders once again. This is the only game that does this crap and it gets old real fast. I'd rather they hotfix this than fixing mining sync. I have limited time I can play each week and having to wait 30 minutes to an hour each time gets old fast.

What an absolutely fun and incredible time, i definitely recommend giving this a try if you're skeptical. It's a really fun, well thought out and overall good time.

The combat is fun, blink range build was the best thing in this game for me though i tried basically everything I could think of. the combat is challenging but done right for the type of game it is, the quest driven progress system was amazing and alot of fun to play through. I'm not huge into building but found myself building an awesome little village for all my people and different types of crafts.

Can't wait for full launch to play again and see the rest of it and keep up with the game as it progresses past that.
Great game! Runs a little rough. I would recommend turning off contact shadows and disabling the cloud saves. This helps it run a little smoother.

Game is fun but a tad difficult to figure out locations and the map needs more details on regions. Also my most hated part about the game is that it doesnt show you how to unlock certain items its not super clear on how to get certain products and they just expect you to make everything but if you dont know the use of something why make it and expend resources. I end up googling a lot to find how to make things. I compare this to icarus a lot since icarus is very complex crafting as well but literally shows you how to make things and has recipes greyed out if you cannot build it yet. Making it more intuitive to be able to make things. Also most missions are insanely difficult still for the wizard to solo. Which forces me to wait for my friends to be on in order to even progress.

I have played this game off and on for multiple years and have loved it since release! I have played sword and shield, ranged, magic and now am working on a 2h build. Starting over from the beginning every time. Building new cavern systems, town and mansions for myself and my companions. I would highly recommend this game to anyone who loves RPG's and sandbox world building.
Enshrouded is like if Zelda Breath of the Wild had a baby with Valheim and teamed up to fight the Last of Us  monsters. It's great. Give it a go.

Enshrouded is one of the best survival RPGs I’ve played in a long time. The world is beautiful, the building system is insanely creative, and the exploration always feels rewarding. Every time I logged in, I found something new hidden loot, massive bosses, or amazing places to build.

The combat is smooth and fun, especially with friends, and the progression system gives you a lot of freedom to create your own playstyle. What really stands out is the atmosphere; the mix of fantasy, mystery, and survival keeps the game feeling immersive the entire time.

Even in early access, the developers clearly care about the game and keep improving it with updates and community feedback. Enshrouded is absolutely worth playing.

Very well fleshed out game from a small group of developers. It's a mixture of adventure with cozy elements that is pretty well balanced in my humble opinion. There are some things that aren't super intuitive - like where to find certain key ingredients dropped from bosses, but I think that is the point of the game. Build, explore, survive. If I had to sum it up, Minecraft for people that like Elden Ring.
This is the most addicting game with so much to do in it that I've played in a long time and I can't wait for the full release.

I have owned this game ever since it first came out, I love it so much and havent barely even explored the world, i am so impatient for it to come to xbox and ps5 so i can also buy it on both of my consoles. I am so excited, and I love this game its by far the best ive played in years.

I’ve been playing this game for quite some time and spent a lot of time exploring many of the newer features and areas released over the past year. While I haven’t yet played the latest update, my experience so far has been very positive. The game offers an enjoyable balance between solo progression and cooperative multiplayer, allowing friends to work together to build villages, towns, or fortified castles while taking on more dangerous challenges for better rewards and gear.

The building system is intuitive and easy to learn, making it simple to create anything from small homes to large-scale structures. Although I did not focus heavily on the story content, the overall gameplay experience consistently remained engaging and enjoyable. The world is large, varied, and well-suited for both casual exploration and long-term progression, and at no point did the experience feel dull or repetitive.

This is sincerely one of the most enjoyable crafting adventure games I've played. I love the way building works, I love being able to claim sections of the world that have been lovingly crafted, and progressing through the world and learning recipes feels great.

I adore this game, and it keeps getting better.

My only gripes are the lack of companions, summons, or pets to fight alongside you. Character creation is also pretty average, with no body size or height options.

Now for what really matters: this game is amazing. The world is huge and beautiful, with tons to do that will keep you busy for hours. It’s great for both family co-op and single-player play. Exploration is fantastic, you can easily spend hours just wandering around and taking everything in.

Think Valheim meets Palworld, but in my opinion, better than both. The base building is on another level compared to most survival games. You can build almost anything your creativity can imagine, though it definitely has a learning curve.

I highly recommend this game and I’m excited to see where the developers take it in the future.

I booted this up thinking I'd play for an hour or two. That was weeks ago.

Enshrouded is one of those games that just gets its hooks into you and refuses to let go. The world of Embervale is genuinely beautiful — the kind of place where you stop mid-exploration just to take in a view, and then realize you've been standing there for five minutes doing nothing because it looks that good. Snowy peaks, dense forests, scorched deserts — every biome feels distinct and alive, and the creeping fog of the Shroud makes venturing into the unknown feel genuinely tense every single time.

But the real reason I can't put this down? The building. I've played a lot of survival games, and nothing — nothing — comes close to the freedom Enshrouded gives you here. You can plant your base anywhere. On a cliffside. On top of a ruin. In the middle of a forest clearing with a perfect view of the valley below. The game doesn't fight you on it. It just lets you build, and the tools it gives you are intuitive enough that your vision actually comes to life instead of turning into a frustrating mess.

I have multiple bases spread across the map now. That's just who I am as a person at this point.

If you have any love for survival games, open worlds, or just the pure satisfaction of building something beautiful in a gorgeous setting — this is a must. One of the best the genre has ever produced.

Pros:
- Incredible realistic looking world to explore
- Relatively well balanced combat/parry windows
- Wide variety of weapons to play with
- Dynamic "POE style" levelling system
- Nice variance in enemies, dungeons, environments, and bossfights
- Transmog/colour change system for Armor

Cons
- Open world exploration is 90% "how do I get up this cliff?
- Early game stamina management is frustrating
- No Transmog for weapons
- Some abilities are incredibly underpowered (sneak attack doesn't work on non-humanoid enemies and bosses) whilst some are basically required imho (double jump has probably shaved off 40/50 hours of "gameplay" trying to get up a cliff)
- Stealth mechanics aren't great (you can be seen through a lot of walls)
- The wolf archetype is insanely OP for some reason, parry window is even throwing off my For Honor sweatlord pal, can't be sneak attacked, head hitbox is tiny..


Suggestions:

- Respawning without 100% stamina is just making the player stand and wait in the safe zone, looking at a green circle fill up for no reason, adds to frustration factor and is just not respecting the time of your players.
-"Out of combat" stamina regen should be 300% what it is, there should be no situation where the player is just standing around doing nothing waiting for a green circle to fill up outside of combat.
- Give players a way to climb/gain elevation.
- Allow the sneak attack ability to be used on every creature type, or remove it from the game.
- Either slow down the Wolf's animations, half it's base HP, or half its damage.. Can't have all three, it's genuinely more difficult than some bosses.

Summary:
Overall it's an incredible game with so much potential

Although I recommend this game, know that the game balance is sketchy,
notably, the Scavenger berserker is really imbalanced and makes me doubt recommending the game.
he's likely going to attack faster then you, attack and chase you faster then you can recover your stamina and has a stun bar that may well break your shield and weapon's durability before you can finish your adventures... enough for me to recommend cheating, and gutting monster power by changing difficulty settings.
The rest of the game, the gear system, the base building, the exploration are all awesome.
the food system, the upgrading of gear, the NPC behavior giving life to your base, it all has a je ne sais quoi.

There just these few monster design that just take your game session from awesome to sewer dredge...
I keep returning for the base building, and keep wanting to uninstall from unfair feeling elite monster balancing.
mind you I only play solo, it's likely that in multiplayer, the monster balance is less retarded.

i really love this game but fix the horrible optimization and lagging - I have decent specs for a computer and it doesnt matter if i rn the game on the lowest settings for everything or max it feels horrible.
After about 2 years in early access this game has not made the progress in progression and combat my friends and I were expecting. A fairly fun game but it feels very samey after a certain point :/

Been playing video games since the original Atari.  Top 5 game.  And i only say top 5 because there have been so many games over almost 50 years.  There's a pretty good argument that this is my all time favorite.  Hurry up with the full release.

1800 hours in.
Lets us climb mountain walls or something. Incredibly frustrating to not have this option when the game has so much verticality. Some locations are a real pain to reach to progress the game further.
This game has taken hours of my life away as soon as I launch it. THIS GAME is absolutely stunning and I cannot wait until full release. If you are on the fence about purchasing, just do it.
A great game for rpg lovers and sandbox worlds

This game has the best base-building I've ever seen, plus a fun adventure RPG to go along with your crafting and building. I absolutely love this game and the developers have been very responsive to their early-access community.

Good game, extremely clunky and bad combat. Unnecessary root motion, animation locks, input blocking.
and it isn't a "get good" situation, it's just poorly implemented.
I like the game for a week or so but after a recent update. I am getting constant lag spikes making it unplayable. I tried all sorts of fixes from other people who had similar issues, but to no avail

I really wanted to love Enshrouded, but it feels like the game is actively fighting against its own potential. Combat feels clunky despite the devs repeatedly “overhauling” it. Animations lock you in place, hitboxes feel inconsistent, ranged combat feels awkward, and dodge timing still feels unreliable. Some improvements were announced, but the core combat still lacks responsiveness compared to other survival RPGs. Then there’s the gearing and progression system, which somehow got more grindy over time. Crafting weapons is mostly pointless because random loot completely overshadows crafted gear. Boss rewards feel underwhelming, progression pacing is inconsistent, and solo balancing often feels miserable. The saddest part is that the building system is simultaneously the game’s best feature and one of its most frustrating. The actual creations you can make are incredible, but basic quality-of-life features players have asked for forever are still missing or poorly implemented: better freecam building, less restrictive movement while building, better snapping behavior, reduced stamina annoyances, easier terrain manipulation, and less janky placement systems. Players have been begging for these improvements for ages and many of them still aren’t properly addressed. It feels like the developers keep reworking systems nobody asked for while longstanding frustrations remain untouched. The roadmap talks about “community wishes,” but many of the most common complaints are still here two years into early access. There’s still a fantastic game buried in here somewhere, the world design, exploration, atmosphere, and building creativity are genuinely great but right now it feels like wasted potential buried under poor optimization, clunky combat, grindy progression, and stubborn design decisions.

This game is unplayable with multiplayer. Friend and I just purchased the game and were excited to play. Hosted a game, friend joins, gets kicked out. Tried again, same thing. Over and over. He tries hosting, now I get kicked anywhere from 15 seconds to 3 minutes into being in the world. Created new world, lowered settings, anything that may help. All fruitless efforts. Horrible experience and waste of an hour trying to get this game to work.

I have better than recommended specs and this game still has a hard time running, the engine struggles hard and turns your pc into a toaster oven.

 Only to look somewhat decent.

Game feels long winded and starts very slowly. Lots of fun possibilities feels like a good amount of effort went into this game. Animations are very lack luster and takes away a great deal of immersion. Feels beyond clunky.

This game is among the best of its genre. If you enjoy this type of game, you will have many hours of content to enjoy. I would like to see more life (npcs/townspeople), greater amount of build customization for combat, higher level cap & double the amount of armor sets & cosmetics, and smarter enemy ai. Overall, fantastic game.

You know this game is good when you end up in, what was supposed to be a quick last quest before logging off, a 2 hour dungeon dying non stop to enemies that are way above your level. As frustrated as we were, we still wanted to keep playing right after. 

Genuinely the best rpg i've played in a while. Great building features, no timed quests so you can actually pace yourself without wanting to cry from demanding npc's, good sized map and the world building is amazing. Highly recommend.

This game speaks for it self. I won't go into detail about it, pick it up for yourself. Enjoy it, the game it worth playing. I've spend 153 hours into this RPG and it was all worth it. Check this out the developer's are very much invested  in this masterpiece. That alone should tell you sometime. take and play on.

Enshrouded is fantastic for most of its journey. Levelling feels well-paced, the base-building system offers a huge amount of flexibility, and the combat is surprisingly solid for a game that also packs in exploration, crafting, and survival mechanics.

The problem starts around level 40–45. Progression shifts heavily towards repetitive chest runs to farm end-game gear. If the RNG isn't on your side, you'll find yourself running the same routes over and over, which quickly becomes tedious and undermines the otherwise excellent pacing.

It's still Early Access, and the foundation is excellent, but the end-game gear grind currently feels more like cheesing the system than engaging with meaningful content. Fix that, and this could be an all-time great survival crafting game.

I played about 15hrs of this game the  starting the day it came out. I was having a blast but then realized there was only about 30hrs of playable content in the initial release version. So, I set the game aside because I wanted to keep playing it when it was more developed. This past few days I picked it back up and I’m now 15hrs into a new play through and I’m having a blast! It really has great derivative components of games like Zelda, Valheim, and even some Elden Ring elements with fighting and bosses being better than those games, yet still is very much its own interesting game It’s is a beautiful open world to explore. The leveling/build system and loot will be familiar if you have played many of the above games and it has a decent complexity without being a miserable grind. Likewise crafting/collection/building can become as complex as you would like but isn’t prohibitively grindy to build decent and interesting structures.  I would highly suggest giving it a go if a combo of the above would interest you. I’m trying to get my wife to play with me, maybe my son.
Coming from playing MMOs this game checks the boxes.  I only wish the action bars were more MMO style.

Fantastic game, incredible graphics and a really intuitive UI. Game is in early access, but it plays like a full release. The Devs really listen to the player, and the game design has really showcased that - i recommend this game to all my RPG-loving friends.

I started playing like a decent person, but then discovered updrafts and double jumps. As a result, 2-3 hours of my life were spent jumping and flying to places where I clearly shouldn't be yet, stealthing to chests with top-tier loot, and taking out 45th-level weapons. And then I could just overwhelm enemies with legendary gear while being level 10. Big like <3

Enshrouded = addiction loop perfected. Build bases, raid dungeons, craft gear, upgrade, repeat. Hundreds of hours later, still finding reasons to play. Combat weighty. Crafting deep without tedium. Exploration relentless, every corner hides secrets, chests, upgrades. Solo or co-op seamless. Never felt forced; always felt rewarding.

Progression never stops. Early game teaches mechanics, mid-game unlocks options, late-game demands mastery. New bosses push challenge. Base customization endless—players build castles, labs, farms, towers. No grind walls; progression matches effort. Difficulty fair; avoidable until ready. Devs keep adding content. Keeps world fresh.

Minor friction doesn't kill it. Performance hiccups on old hardware. Rare clipping bugs. Doesn't matter, core loop transcends jank. Content sustains. Not padding. Not tedium. Pure progression satisfaction. If survival + exploration + crafting clicks for you, this hits different.

absolutely fantastic world building! the story, which is read only basically, is great! the exploration is amazing! starting off in this massive world is rough but once you start to understand all the mechanics it becomes very rewarding! i highly recommend this game to anyone who loves a huge map to explore and build in!

Do you like exploration of dark dungeons? Do you enjoy fighting against monsterous mobs? Do you like revealing the truth of the ancient past? Do you like the freedom to build crazy creations in an open world? If you answered yes to any of these questions then this is the game for you.  Want to build an impossibly big castle in the sky, you can do that in Enshrouded! If you like creating haunted houses with hidden doors and pathways, then this is the game for you!  The building is super addictive, you can tunnel your way through dirt and even alter the terrain too! The tools, weapons, crafting is really good too. I would give this game a 4/5 stars for doing things right and continuing to perfect it with each new patch! If you loved Valheim, but wished there was no build height limit...welcome to Enshrouded..the only limits are your imagination. You also can build water ways! That moat you always wanted for your build...completely possible!

First off I want to start this review by saying i absolutely love this game and am enjoying the heck out of that. With that out of the way I do have a bit of criticism making areas have a ton of enemies and making every enemies regular hit a knock back/interrupt is a little lazy when it comes to the difficulty. Make enemies have different mechanics make enemies do more than just bombard you with hit after hit after hit just to interrupt or knock back, you flood the hollows with tons of enemies which makes it more annoying than difficult. It's becoming a slog just to do some of the content.
One of the best survival base building game! So much content, lots of building options and very fun in co-op. Definitely recommend!
I don't know why it took me so long to try this game out but at the end of the day I'm glad I did. highly recommend this game.

its one Of the best openworld games i've played, and i've played a lot of them
if you enjoy fantasy or DnD you should check it out, it combines both of them
and (atleast at the Time im Playing it) it has great Bosses, A great Combatsystem, A great Build system and incredible attention to Detail

My wife and I usually play MMOs like WoW and GW2, and Enshrouded has been a refreshing change. It combines Minecraft-style building with quests, progression, and boss fights that feel more like an MMO. Setting up a server was easy, and being able to play with up to 16 friends makes it even better. We've had a lot of fun and would definitely recommend it.

I have spent an inordinate amount of time in this game building to my heart's desire. All the amazing updates made throughout the pre-release time have been incredible and added so much to the experience.

I started playing this game with my friends when it first came out, and it was quite a lot of fun. I spent about 80 hours in the game building, exploring and fighting with my friends, and it seemed like the game was a really good multiplayer RPG, so when I heard my MOM playing it ALONE and enjoying it, I got a bit curious.

I've noe spent quite a lot of time, some of it frustrating, some of it exciting and some relaxed. 
The good is that the world is now gigantic, although it quickly feels very small once you get a good glider: A journey that used to take 10-15 minutes now takes 1 minute, which means that you often have to force yourself to actually go that route to experience the mysteries of the world. 
Playing alone, I felt like a lot of combat had to be cheesed in some way. I really enjoy building, however, I wished that there was more space for building in some of the regions.

Overall it is pretty fun!
